element_introduction_order.mastery_context: NPC interaction, item trading, inn rest, rune collection hub
Enemy tracking, navigation, threat assessment during overworld exploration
Avoidance tactics, combat initiation, resource management
Attack, defend, spell casting, item use, flee mechanics
Dueling other apprentices, forming partnerships, competing for runes
Persistent threat, randomized traitor apprentice mechanic
Weapon and armor upgrades via gold accumulation
Identity twist, power absorption mechanic, final confrontation

typical_tension_triggers: Enemy radar dots approaching on crystal ball
Rival apprentice duels for rune control
Stat disadvantage in localized NA version
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based: safe town respite periods alternating with escalating enemy-infested overworld segments; tension intensifies mid-game with rival encounters and bounty hunter pressure; climax at endgame identity twist and time-displacement to ancient world

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden items searchable in towns; chests findable in overworld/dungeons
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Melenam ruins (explored early as ruins) revealed late-game as technologically advanced origin city; non-linear town visitation order allows player routing choice

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate introduction to radar avoidance and basic combat; character selection determines early-game power level; rivals available as allies
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating rune-holder difficulty; bounty hunter persistence increases threat; rival apprentices grow stronger; stat-disadvantage accumulation (NA localization)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Severe difficulty spike: Gorsia identity reveal and power absorption; time displacement to past world; multi-town traversal to Melenam; final sealed rune confrontation
notable_spikes: Bounty hunter Pison persistent encounters mid-game
Lemele/Gorsia endgame reveal and lightning attack
Past-world Melenam navigation with rogue Tetsujin robot

notable_level_patterns.description: Town sequences serve as narrative/resource breaks where players interact with NPCs, purchase upgrades, recruit allies, rest at inns, and receive quest direction toward next rune
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Starting town; any city between rune hunts

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeon or stronghold sequences focused on defeating specific tyrant possessing a rune; each rune-holder presents distinct combat challenge (wizard, king, dragon variants)
Crystal ball radar system creates real-time threat avoidance gameplay between towns; enemies move rapidly and numerous, requiring tactical navigation and positioning
Fixed or triggered meetings with other playable characters in towns allowing duels for runes or partnership formation; outcomes affect available team compositions
Post-twist sequences in past-world Ticondera (5,000 years prior); reveals technological origins and Gorsia's true history; multi-stage progression to Melenam discovery
